meta.name: Octupie
meta.tab-title: Octupie | Your viral-content researcher & script-writing agent
meta.suited-secondary: Brand blue (#014CE3 primary, #4C61FF accent) over white surfaces, with deep navy (#0B1430) text. White + brand blue keep visual identity continuous across themes.
meta.illustration-policy: All product-UI illustrations (hero-visualization, feature-illustrations, step-illustrations) stay DARK in light mode by design. Their --illus-* CSS vars are pinned to dark values in both themes so the embedded product previews read as dark cards on the white page. This is intentional, not a bug.

token.name: background (page)
token.role: html + body, section backgrounds, footer strip.

token.name: card
token.role: Default card, nav pill, feature card, niche card, faq item, waitlist form container.

token.name: card-hover
token.role: Feature card hover, how-it-works step card hover.

token.name: card-elevated
token.role: How-it-works active step card (bluewashed white).

token.name: panel
token.role: FAQ tab selected pill, pre launch / eyebrow pill bg, problem chip bg.

token.name: input-bg
token.role: Waitlist name + email inputs.

token.name: mobile-menu-bg
token.role: Nav dropdown drawer.

token.name: foreground
token.role: Headings, primary text, wordmark 'Octupie', CTA labels over white.

token.name: muted-foreground
token.role: Subcopy, muted paragraphs, stat descriptions, faq answers.

token.name: faint-foreground
token.role: Eyebrow small labels, mobile eyebrow, footer small text.

token.name: faintest-foreground
token.role: Footnote strip, copyright.

token.name: placeholder
token.role: Input placeholder.

token.name: border
token.role: Default card, section, nav pill border.

token.name: border-strong
token.role: Hover card border, input focus-within.

token.name: border-brand
token.role: Active step card border, focused input.

token.name: border-brand-strong
token.role: Active step pill outline, selected state.

token.name: divider
token.role: Hairline dividers, .divider-line.

token.name: grid-line
token.role: Hero .bg-grid-overlay 48x48 grid.

token.name: brand-primary (CTA)
token.role: Waitlist button fill, active step pill fill, primary link text on light.

token.name: brand-accent-1
token.role: Hover CTA fill, ring, glow pulse, dots, hover accent bar.

token.name: eyebrow-accent
token.role: 'What you actually get', 'Why Octupie', 'Pre launch' label text on light. Swaps to #9BB2FF on dark.

token.name: link-hover
token.role: Eyebrow / link hover on light (goes darker, not lighter).

token.name: brand-fill-15
token.role: Pre launch pill bg, 'Octupie fixes it' badge bg (before minimal rewrite).

token.name: brand-fill-30
token.role: Pre launch pill border.

token.name: destructive-fg
token.role: Form error message text.

token.name: problem-dot
token.role: Why Octupie pain bullet dot (red-400/70).

token.name: grid-line-rgb
token.role: Used as rgb(var(--grid-line-rgb) / ALPHA) for the .bg-grid-overlay and .divider-line utilities.

token.name: divider-rgb
token.role: Same pattern for .divider-line.

token.name: page-bg-transparent
token.role: End stop for .gradient-radial-bottom so the glow fades into the white page.

token.name: illus-bg-start
token.role: SVG illustration gradient top stop. INTENTIONALLY dark on light theme.

token.name: illus-bg-end
token.role: SVG illustration gradient bottom stop. Dark on light theme.

token.name: illus-chip-start
token.role: SVG chip gradient top.

token.name: illus-chip-end
token.role: SVG chip gradient bottom.

token.name: illus-text
token.role: Text inside illustrations.

typography.note: Identical to dark spec. Host Grotesk 500 for headings, Inter for body. Hero h1 uses fluid clamp(32px, 9vw, 40px).

radii.note: Identical to dark spec. rounded-full pills, rounded-2xl cards, rounded-3xl large CTA card.

shadow.name: card-lift
shadow.role: New on light: subtle shadow on every card where dark relied on contrast alone. Applied to feature cards, niche cards, faq items, why octupie cards.

shadow.name: cta-primary
shadow.role: Primary CTA button. Unchanged across themes. Highlight on blue reads cleaner on white.

shadow.name: nav-scrolled
shadow.role: Nav pill shadow after scroll on light (replaces 0 8px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.35) from dark).

shadow.name: faq-tab-active
shadow.role: Selected FAQ tab inner light-to-shadow (reversed vs dark).

breakpoints.note: Unchanged: sm 640, md 768, lg 1024, xl 1280, 2xl 1536. Mobile first.

motion.rule: All keyframes are theme-safe: only opacity and transform are animated.
`glow-pulse` ring stays rgba(76,97,255,*) (brand blue) in both themes.
Marquee track uses .gpu-layer, duplicated children, 60s linear infinite.

rule.css: background: var(--background); /* #FFFFFF in light */ color: var(--foreground); /* #0B1430 in light */

utility.name: .bg-grid-overlay
utility.desc: In light mode renders rgb(11 20 48 / 0.05) grid on white (faintly visible navy dots).

utility.name: .divider-line
utility.desc: In light mode renders rgb(11 20 48 / 0.10) gradient hairline.

utility.name: .gradient-radial-bottom
utility.desc: Fades from rgba(1,76,227,0.35) into rgba(255,255,255,0) on light, so the hero blue glow blends into the white page.

utility.name: .card-gradient-border
utility.desc: Unchanged. The rgba(1,76,227,*) pseudo-border reads on both themes.

utility.name: .glow-pulse
utility.desc: Unchanged. Brand blue ring visible on both themes.

toggle.shape: 36px round button, sun/moon inline SVG, useSyncExternalStore mount guard
toggle.placement: inside Nav pill next to WaitlistButton

theme-plumbing.ssr-safety: html + body both carry suppressHydrationWarning. Initial SSR renders in light markup (no .dark class); next-themes may flip class post-mount if user previously chose dark.

paired-class-pattern.intro: Every hardcoded dark Tailwind class in the original design is migrated to a paired light + dark utility. The component renders the light utility when html has no .dark class, the dark utility when .dark is present.

pair.note: Eyebrow color swap: lilac pale on dark, rich brand blue on light.
Slightly stronger accent wash on white so the tint is visible.

section.pill: bg-white/95, border border-black/10, shadow 0 8px 30px rgba(11,20,48,0.10). NO backdrop-blur.
section.wordmark: OctupieLogo (unchanged SVG) + span text-[#0B1430] font-heading text-xl.
section.theme-toggle: h-9 w-9 round button, border-black/10, bg-[#0B1430]/5 hover:bg-[#0B1430]/10, sun icon when isDark (so clicking = back to light), moon icon when light.
section.mobile-menu: fixed inset-x-4 top-20, rounded-2xl border-black/10 bg-white p-6, link list text-[#0B1430]/90.

section.wrapper: Same overflow-hidden pt-36 pb-20 md:pt-44 md:pb-28.
section.announce-pill: bg-white/5 style becomes bg-[#0B1430]/5, border border-black/10, text-[#0B1430]/80. InstagramIcon + LinkedInIcon unchanged (self-contained colored tiles).
section.headline: font-heading text-[clamp(32px,9vw,40px)] ... text-[#0B1430], tracking-tight, break-words, hyphens-auto.
section.form-slot: WaitlistForm ctaLabel "Join the Waitlist". Note: inputs now use bg-[#F5F7FB] + border-black/10 + text-[#0B1430] + placeholder-[#0B1430]/45; focus:border-[#4C61FF].

product-frame.card: INTENTIONALLY DARK in light mode: border border-white/10 bg-[#040E22]/90 p-3 backdrop-blur-sm. dark variant: bg-[#040E22]/60. The card hugs a dark illustration so it does not float.

section.eyebrow: text-sm font-medium uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-[#014CE3]. Copy: "What you actually get".
section.card: rounded-2xl border border-black/10 bg-white p-6, shadow-[0_1px_2px_rgba(11,20,48,0.04)], hover:bg-[#F5F7FB] hover:border-[#4C61FF]/40.
section.illustration-frame: INTENTIONALLY DARK: border-white/5 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#0a152c] to-[#040E22]. Same in both themes. Wraps the dark FeatureIllustration SVG.
section.title: font-heading text-[#0B1430].

section.preview-pane: INTENTIONALLY DARK on light: border-white/10 bg-[#040E22] p-3 wraps the StepIllustration SVG. Eyebrow inside pane: text-[#4C61FF]. Title: text-white (because the pane is dark).

section.section-heading: "Built for short form creators across every niche" text-[#0B1430]. No eyebrow pill, no subtitle (removed).
section.marquee-edge-fade: left: from-white to-transparent. right: from-white to-transparent.
section.niche-card: rounded-2xl border border-black/10 bg-white p-5, shadow-[0_1px_2px_rgba(11,20,48,0.04)]. Icon badge: h-10 w-10 rounded-xl border-black/10 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#EEF2FA] to-[#FFFFFF], icon color text-[#014CE3]. Label text-[#0B1430]. Blurb text-[#0B1430]/65.

section.eyebrow: text-sm font-medium uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-[#014CE3]. Copy: "Why Octupie". Matches Features eyebrow style exactly.
section.h2: text-[#0B1430]. "Built to fix what research / tools leave unfinished".
section.card: rounded-2xl border border-black/10 bg-white p-6, shadow-[0_1px_2px_rgba(11,20,48,0.04)], hover:border-[#4C61FF]/40. Minimal: no badges, no index, no eyebrows, no detail paragraphs.
section.pain-line: text-sm text-[#0B1430]/60, preceded by a 1.5x1.5 red-dot (bg-red-400/70).
section.fix-headline: font-heading text-[20px] md:text-[22px] text-[#0B1430], preceded by a 1.5x1.5 brand-dot (bg-[#4C61FF]).
section.removed: Problem/fix split, "x" red badge, connector arrow chip, "Octupie fixes it" logo badge, index "0N", footnote legend strip, blur blob backdrops, hairline divider above section.

section.frame: rounded-3xl border border-black/10 bg-white px-8 py-16 md:px-16 md:py-20.
section.bg-image: footerBg webp rendered at opacity-60; gradient overlay fades to var(--background) (white) via .gradient-radial-bottom style mapping.

contact-link.email-line: text-[#0B1430]/65 hover:text-[#014CE3]. Copy: "vishnu@octupie.com". Displayed directly below the primary link.

section.tab-switcher: border-black/10 bg-white p-1. Tab default: text-[#0B1430]/70 hover:text-[#0B1430]. Tab selected: bg-[#EEF2FA] text-[#0B1430] shadow-[inset_0_2px_4px_rgba(11,20,48,0.08)].

section.contact-us: Mailto link label "Contact Us" + second visible list item "vishnu@octupie.com", both href mailto:vishnu@octupie.com?subject=Octupie%20inquiry.

component.name: WaitlistButton
component.primary: bg-[#014CE3] text-white, shadow-[inset_0_1px_0_rgba(255,255,255,0.24),0_8px_24px_rgba(1,76,227,0.40)], hover:bg-[#4C61FF]. Unchanged across themes.

component.name: WaitlistForm
component.input: rounded-full border border-black/10 bg-[#F5F7FB] px-5 py-3 text-sm text-[#0B1430] placeholder:text-[#0B1430]/45 outline-none transition focus:border-[#4C61FF] focus:bg-white.
component.success-pill: rounded-full border-[#4C61FF]/30 bg-[#4C61FF]/15 px-5 py-3 text-sm text-[#014CE3], check icon + "You are on the list. We will be in touch soon.".

component.name: Brandmark / Wordmark
component.logo: OctupieLogo SVG unchanged. Brand-gradient rounded square with 8 white dots + white core. Reads equally well on both themes.

component.name: ThemeToggle
component.icon: Inline SVG sun (light-is-active) or moon (dark-is-active). Stroke currentColor text-[#0B1430].
component.mount-guard: useSyncExternalStore pattern. Pre-mount defaults to isDark=false so SSR light matches client light.

component.name: NicheIcon / InstagramIcon / LinkedInIcon
component.note: Unchanged. Niche icons use stroke=currentColor and inherit the card text color. Instagram and LinkedIn are self-contained colored tiles (gradient + white marks) so they render identically on both themes.

component.name: Illustrations (feature / step / hero-visualization)
component.note: PINNED DARK. Their --illus-* CSS vars are set to dark values in both :root and .dark. SVG gradients, grid, text fills are all dark palette. Intentional: product UI previews stay recognizable and the surrounding wrappers are also dark so there are no floating dark slabs.

copy-invariants.rule: No em dashes (U+2014) or en dashes (U+2013).
No fabricated testimonials. Niche archetypes only.
"Pre launch" pill and the "Octupie has not launched yet" paragraph are REMOVED from social proof. Only the headline remains above the marquee.
Why Octupie eyebrow uses the Features eyebrow style (plain uppercase text, no pill, no dot).

Contact email: vishnu@octupie.com. Mailto links include ?subject=Octupie%20inquiry. The email is displayed as visible text both in FAQ (below "Contact us →") and in Footer (second Company column link).
Tab title uses pipe separator: "Octupie | ...". No dash.

reproduction.step: Load Host Grotesk + Inter. Set body bg #FFFFFF, color #0B1430, antialiased.
Install next-themes, mount ThemeProvider with attribute="class", defaultTheme="light", enableSystem=false, disableTransitionOnChange. Add suppressHydrationWarning on html + body.
In globals.css, put the LIGHT token set in :root and the DARK token set in .dark. body reads var(--background) / var(--foreground).
For every component, migrate hardcoded dark values to paired utilities using the paired-class table. Base class renders light, dark: variant renders dark.
Illustrations: define --illus-* CSS vars once with dark values and consume them inside every SVG via the stop-color=var(--illus-...) attribute on each gradient stop. Do NOT split these across themes.
Add ThemeToggle (36px round moon/sun) to the nav pill. Use useSyncExternalStore for mount detection so SSR matches the default light render.
Swap eyebrow #9BB2FF to #014CE3 on light (plain uppercase text, no pill wrapper) to keep hierarchy.
Apply subtle 0 1px 2px rgba(11,20,48,0.04) shadow to every card where dark relied on contrast.
Nav, CTA card, hero product-frame wrappers must STAY dark in light mode when they directly host a dark illustration. This prevents floating dark slabs.
Test: typecheck, lint, next build. Toggle theme, verify localStorage persistence. Resize 375 and 1280 both modes.

stay-dark-in-light-mode.element: Hero product frame (hero.tsx outer card around HeroVisualization).
Features card illustration frame (the inner gradient box, not the outer card).
How-it-works preview pane (right column wrapping StepIllustration).
All inline SVG illustration content (feature-illustrations, step-illustrations, hero-visualization).
Primary WaitlistButton (blue fill with white text, same in both themes).
Marquee card icon gradient when rendered against the white card (colors adjust but badge itself is a blue-to-white gradient on white).
stay-dark-in-light-mode.rationale: These elements either host dark illustration content and would leave a visible black rectangle on white, or are brand-colored CTAs whose contrast works on any background.
